title: Prenatal exposure to perfluorinated chemicals and behavioral or coordination problems at age 7 years.
ehr_evidence: The study used data from the Danish National Birth Cohort (DNBC) and validated questionnaires (SDQ).
summary: A longitudinal study investigating the association between maternal levels of PFOA and PFOS during pregnancy and neurodevelopmental/behavioral outcomes in children.
key_findings: Prenatal exposure to PFCs was associated with reduced fetal growth.
No significant association between in utero exposure to PFOA and PFOS and neurologic development at 6 and 18 months of age.
population: Children in the Danish National Birth Cohort
limitations: Previous studies showed no association at 6 and 18 months
